title: The Rule of 72: How Long to Double Your Money at Any Return
description: A 15th-century mathematical shortcut reveals exactly when your investments will 2x. Find out why the 7% and 10% rules change everything.

schemas.headline: The Rule of 72: How Long to Double Your Money at Any Return Rate
schemas.description: A 15th-century mathematical shortcut reveals exactly when your investments will 2x. Find out why the 7% and 10% rules change everything.

acceptedAnswer.text: The Rule of 72 is a simple mathematical formula used to estimate how many years it will take to double an investment given a fixed annual rate of return. You simply divide 72 by your annual return rate.

mainEntity.name: How long to double money at 7%?

acceptedAnswer.text: Using the Rule of 72, if you earn a 7% annual return, it will take approximately 10.3 years to double your money (72 / 7 = 10.28).

mainEntity.name: How long to double money at 10%?

acceptedAnswer.text: At a 10% annual return rate, it will take exactly 7.2 years for your money to double according to the Rule of 72 (72 / 10 = 7.2).

mainEntity.name: How accurate is the rule of 72 calculator?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Rule of 72 calculator is very accurate for interest rates between 6% and 10%. Outside of this range, it provides a very close estimate, but the exact logarithmic formula is required for perfect precision.

mainEntity.name: When does the rule of 72 break down?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Rule of 72 breaks down and becomes less accurate at very high return rates (e.g., above 20%) or very low return rates (e.g., below 2%). For these extremes, the Rule of 69.3 or exact logarithmic formulas provide better estimates.
